---
title: "About"
image: profile.jpg
about:
  template: jolla
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/rabindranath-duran-pons-1682591ab/
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/rabin0208

---

## About This Blog

This blog explores how data science is changing the way we understand football. It presents the work from our UBC Master of Data Science capstone project with the Trilemma Foundation — turning millions of on-pitch events into tactical insights that scouts, analysts, and fans can actually use.

## Who This Blog Is For

This blog is written for football fans and sports enthusiasts who:

- Are curious about how analytics work in modern football
- Have heard terms like clustering or machine learning but want plain-language explanations
- Want to understand player roles and consistency without writing SQL
- Are interested in how clubs and national teams use data to make better decisions

No data science degree required — just curiosity and a love for the game.

## About the Project

This work was completed as part of the DSCI 591 Capstone at UBC, in partnership with the Trilemma Foundation. Our team — Ngoc Minh Quan Hoang, Teem Kwong, Rabindranath Duran Pons, and Li Pu — built a fully automated analytics platform on top of StatsBomb open data, covering 16 competitions and over 12 million on-ball events.

The platform delivers player archetype labels, club-vs-national consistency scores, an interactive Looker Studio dashboard, and a plain-English chatbot — all reproducible from a single `docker compose up`.

## About Me

I'm a Master of Data Science student who loves sports. This blog is where I share how analytics and data science are changing the game we love, explained in a way that doesn't require a math degree.

Thanks for stopping by — let's talk football!
